MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-25-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WHOLE FOODS MARKET CALIFORNIA, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication.

Defendant Whole Foods Market California, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff fails to show that the concrete floor where plaintiff fell constituted a dangerous condition. There is no evidence that the floor was a dangerous condition when it was in its dry, unadulterated state. Plaintiff's expert (Zachary Moore) states that the floor had an average dry slip resistance of .73, which exceeds the industry standard of .5. (Moore Dec., 7-8.) There is also no evidence that the floor was dangerously wet on the day of the occurrence. Moore assumes that the floor was wet even though there is no evidence to support that assumption. The adduced evidence shows: (1) plaintiff does not know what caused her to fall (Smith Depo., 35:23-36:1); (2) plaintiff did not see water or ice on the floor and her clothes were not wet (Smith Depo., 50:6-18); and (3) defendant's employee who examined the floor after the incident did not see anything on the floor that could have caused the slip (Douglas Dec., 2.). (See Peralta v. The Vons Companies, Inc. (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 1030 [granting summary judgment where the plaintiff did not see any liquid on the floor.])

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
